mechanism: $2T+ annual Treasury issuance raises term premium at the long end while the short end remains anchored to the Taylor Rule, producing negative 2Y-SPX correlation and positive 30Y-SPX correlation. The fiscal supply channel operates through the long end, not the short end.
regime_dependence: Strongest when fiscal deficits are large (>5% GDP) and the central bank maintains short-end credibility. Weakens if the Fed loses credibility (both ends go positive) or if fiscal consolidation occurs (long-end term premium falls).
